intro: Chaetanthera frayjorgensis is a plant species native to the Patagonia region of South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae and is commonly known as Patagonian cineraria. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Chaetanthera frayjorgensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
